title: Elections for the government's chief executive
subtitle: Data by the Lexical Index of Electoral Democracy.
caption:
Elections for the government's chief executive, 2025
Data by the Lexical Index of Electoral Democracy.

This world map displays the status of elections for the government's chief executive in 2025, categorizing countries into those with such elections and those without. The visualization uses a binary color scheme where blue indicates the presence of elections for the chief executive, and orange indicates their absence, with grey hatching denoting areas where no data is available. The blue coloration covers the vast majority of the global landmass, including the entirety of North and South America, Australia, and most of Europe and sub-Saharan Africa. Specific nations falling into this category include the United States, Brazil, the United Kingdom, France, Germany, India, and South Africa. Conversely, the orange shading identifies a distinct cluster of countries where the chief executive is not elected, concentrated primarily in the Middle East, North Africa, and parts of Asia. This group includes China, Russia, Saudi Arabia, Iran, Egypt, Libya, Algeria, Sudan, and Morocco. Greenland appears with grey hatching, signifying a lack of data for that territory. The geographic distribution shows a clear divide, with the "No" category forming a contiguous band stretching from the Atlantic coast of Africa across the Arabian Peninsula to the Pacific coast of Asia. The "Yes" category dominates the Western Hemisphere, Oceania, and large portions of the Eastern Hemisphere outside the aforementioned band. The pattern visible on the map shows that elections for the government's chief executive are the standard arrangement globally, with a specific concentration of non-elective systems in North Africa, the Middle East, and Central and East Asia.
